channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-17712] recovery-small test_157: multiop failed with not SIGBUS
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for Vitaly Fertman <vitaly_fertman@xyratex.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/0b7d7fdd-c88e-4906-9028-43e152ad797d

test_157 failed with the following error:

multiop failed with not SIGBUS

Test session details:
clients: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/103597 - 5.14.0-362.18.1.el9_3.x86_64
servers: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/103597 - 4.18.0-513.18.1.el8_lustre.x86_64

<<Please provide additional information about the failure here>>

VVVVVVV DO NOT REMOVE LINES BELOW, Added by Maloo for auto-association VVVVVVV
recovery-small test_157 - multiop failed with not SIGBUS
item.summary: recovery-small test_157: multiop failed with not SIGBUS

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
The question is whether the test has to fail with SIGBUS, or that it probably fails with SIGBUS but the test is a bit racy and may not report any error at all in some cases?

Yes, error is not reported if connection manages to get restored before __do_page_fault() retries handle_mm_fault() after VM_FAULT_RETRY is returned by ll_fault() in first attempt:

00000080:00000008:1.0:1730404243.986512:0:47443:0:(llite_mmap.c:464:ll_fault()) COMPLETED: [0x200000404:0x43:0x0]: vma=ffff8e2a184a6658 start=0x7fdbe1ba2000 end=0x7fdbe1ba3000 vm_flags=0x80000fb idx=0 vmf_flags=0xa54: rc=1024 <<< VM_FAULT_RETRY
..
00000100:02000000:1.0:1730404243.986831:0:47463:0:(import.c:1690:ptlrpc_import_recovery_state_machine()) lustre-OST0000-osc-ffff8e2a02d8c800: Connection restored to 10.240.25.194@tcp (at 10.240.25.194@tcp)
..
00000080:00000008:1.0:1730404243.989492:0:47443:0:(llite_mmap.c:464:ll_fault()) COMPLETED: [0x200000404:0x43:0x0]: vma=ffff8e2a184a6658 start=0x7fdbe1ba2000 end=0x7fdbe1ba3000 vm_flags=0x80000fb idx=0 vmf_flags=0xa74: rc=512 <<< VM_FAULT_LOCKED, page fault succeeded

In case of failure connection gets restored after __do_page_fault() gave up already:

00000080:00000008:0.0:1730404239.434547:0:46734:0:(llite_mmap.c:464:ll_fault()) COMPLETED: [0x200000404:0x41:0x0]: vma=ffff8e2a182f82b8 start=0x7f1a536c3000 end=0x7f1a536c4000 vm_flags=0x80000fb idx=0 vmf_flags=0xa54: rc=1024 <<< VM_FAULT_RETRY
..
00000080:00000008:0.0:1730404239.434617:0:46734:0:(llite_mmap.c:464:ll_fault()) COMPLETED: [0x200000404:0x41:0x0]: vma=ffff8e2a182f82b8 start=0x7f1a536c3000 end=0x7f1a536c4000 vm_flags=0x80000fb idx=0 vmf_flags=0xa74: rc=2 <<< VM_FAULT_SIGBUS, __do_page_fault() does not retry handle_mm_fault()
 ..
00000100:02000000:1.0:1730404239.434879:0:46754:0:(import.c:1690:ptlrpc_import_recovery_state_machine()) lustre-OST0000-osc-ffff8e2a02d8c800: Connection restored to 10.240.25.194@tcp (at 10.240.25.194@tcp)

 So, it is ok for the test to accept 0 and 135.

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56884/
Subject: LU-17712 tests: allow multiop to return 0 in recovery-small/157
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 44b81d53f2d05b6f5d92ad46baa5d56c57f2146d

"Jian Yu <yujian@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56884
Subject: LU-17712 tests: allow multiop to return 0 in recovery-small/157
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: ba41fd427d5895760cd7596268518e329ec955c9

Jian Yu can you please submit a separate patch that changes the check to "(( rc == 0 || rc == 135 ))" so that this test can pass consistently.

Sure, Andreas.

To

I've added a tiny bit of debugging into patch https://review.whamcloud.com/56829 "LU-18402 tests: skip recovery-small/

Unknown macro: {153,154a,157}

for older MDS" to print what error code is being returned.

To extract my comment from Gerrit, where it is easily lost:

It looks like the client was evicted, but there was no error reported:

 Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: == recovery-small test 157: eviction during mmaped i/o === 04:08:47 (1730434127)
 LustreError: lustre-OST0000-osc-ffff898542ef5800: This client was evicted by lustre-OST0000; in progress operations using this service will fail.
 Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: recovery-small test_157: @@@@@@ FAIL: multiop failed with 0, not SIGBUS (135)

vsaveliev, is it OK if this eviction didn't cause multiop to fail with SIGBUS?

In that case, it would be possible to change the test case to allow ((rc == 0 || rc = 135)) so that the test can pass consistently, but I don't know if that is breaking the whole point of the test...

The question is whether the test has to fail with SIGBUS, or that it probably fails with SIGBUS but the test is a bit racy and may not report any error at all in some cases?

Failed enforced test review-dne-subtest-change on RHEL 9.3 / x86_64
uploaded by Trevis Autotest 2 from trevis-131vm1: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/946d9310-0b9b-4cab-9175-440c18847740
ran 3 tests. 1 tests failed: recovery-small.

This session passed 104 iterations of test_157() in a row until it again "failed" with rc=0, so it seems like a racy test. I think from the point of view of the original bug (thread stuck on endless retry when an mmap RPC has an error) this should still be considered "success" since the mmap did not hang?

yujian can you please submit a separate patch that changes the check to "(( rc == 0 || rc == 135 ))" so that this test can pass consistently.

Hi vsaveliev
Could you please advise?

I've added a tiny bit of debugging into patch https://review.whamcloud.com/56829 "LU-18402 tests: skip recovery-small/{153,154a,157} for older MDS" to print what error code is being returned. It probably needs someone to review the kernel debug logs to see why it isn't failing with SIGBUS. Possibly it is OK to fail with another error code?

This subtest was added in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/51505 "LU-16935 llite: avoid hopeless i/o repeats" (commit v2_15_61-146-gf5564c35ed on 2024-03-23) and has been failing intermittently since that time (117 failures in 7 months).

It failed after 33 iterations with ONLY_REPEAT in session:
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/ab072d4d-9370-4bc2-8b61-2ac1b25cfc2b
+1 on master branch: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/85280ba9-18e5-4bea-a37d-3d70f4e84a7f
The failure occurred 90 times in the last 6 months.
+1 on master branch: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/f1f4ab33-45ce-4c2b-a3af-398bfdb3727b
